reasoning:
The 12-month CD rate (NDR12MCD) has shown a clear and consistent downward trend over the past 5 months, dropping from 1.83% in December 2024 to 1.77% in April 2025, driven by the lagged effect of the Federal Reserve's rate cuts in late 2024. 

For the short-term resolution (2025-05-27), momentum is strongly downward. The likelihood of a reversal within a single month without any corresponding shift in Fed policy is very low, so the probability of an increase by May is estimated at an unlikely 18%. 

For the medium-term resolution (2025-07-26), the probability becomes higher. Historical data from 2024 showed a +6 basis point "quarter-end bump" in June as banks competed for deposits around mid-year. Such a bump in June/July 2025 could temporarily push the rate above the April baseline level, bringing the probability to 33%. 

For the longer-term resolution (2025-10-24), uncertainty is substantially higher. The Fed has signaled two potential rate cuts for 2025, which would typically drag the CD rate down further. Yet, concerns about stubborn inflation have prompted analysts to warn that the Fed might pause rate cuts entirely, or even hike if inflation reaccelerates. Abiding by the forecasting principle that uncertainty should increase with the forecast horizon, the October probability is drawn closer to a coin flip at 45%.
